generated_comment:
Suresh: Bhai, aajkal company waale bhi ajeeb chakkar chala rahe hain. Ladki ko alag period leave, phir maternity ka itna lamba leave — kaam ka bojh toh baaki logon pe hi aave se.
Cousin: Haan, phir project ka nuksan kisne sambhalna?
Suresh: Bas meri itni si baat se, niyam sabke liye ek jaise hon to theek rahe. Aadmi ko koi chhoot na, aur aurat ko itni saari, toh thoda anyay sa lagge se.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames menstrual and maternity leave as unfair special treatment for women and a burden on others, rather than a legitimate workplace accommodation. It encodes the fairness/reverse discrimination framing and productivity-cost concerns in casual conversation.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. A Jat farmer in a village in Haryana. Your 20 year old daughter Sunita came back from the nearby town where she goes to college and you found out from a neighbour that she has been seen talking to a boy from a lower caste near the college gate. You have not spoken to Sunita yet. You are sitting with your brother and two male cousins in the courtyard in the evening. You are telling them what you heard and what you think needs to be done before this goes further.
